title: desktop: send_chat broadcasts plaintext ClearMessage with no MLS/HPKE — relay sees message content
description:
## Bug

In desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs send_chat():

    let payload = serde_json::to_vec(&ClearMessage::Chat { text }).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
    let padded = pad(&payload).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
    let rpc = JsonRpcRequest::new(next_request_id(), rpc_methods::BROADCAST,
        BroadcastParams { payload: padded })...
    tx.send(rpc).await...

This sends a BROADCAST with the raw (padded but unencrypted) ClearMessage payload. There is no MLS encryption layer and no HPKE sealing.

Padding provides traffic analysis resistance but not confidentiality. The relay sees the plaintext chat content.

## Impact

The Tauri desktop client provides no end-to-end encryption. The relay (or any relay operator) can read all messages sent from the desktop app. This contradicts the system's stated security model.

Note: This is analogous to the existing issues nie-jq8m.7 (FFI) and nie-po5v.18 (TUI /dm), but for the Tauri desktop client specifically.

## Fix

The desktop client needs to integrate MLS encryption before sending messages, consistent with the TUI client's send_chat() implementation.
close_reason: send_chat now encrypts via MLS when group is active, matching TUI behavior
